title: Stealth & OPSEC Techniques for Penetration Testing
description: Comprehensive reference for stealth techniques and operational security (OPSEC) practices during authorized penetration tests.

techniques.name: Tor Network Routing
techniques.description: Route all traffic through the Tor onion network to anonymize source IP. Provides multi-hop encryption and IP obfuscation.
techniques.notes: Exit node IP may be on public Tor exit lists. Use bridges for higher stealth.

techniques.name: Proxychains
techniques.description: Force any application to use SOCKS/HTTP proxies by intercepting connect syscalls. Supports chaining multiple proxies.
techniques.notes: Use 'dynamic_chain' mode in proxychains.conf for resilience to dead proxies.

techniques.name: Torsocks Wrapper
techniques.description: Library-level Tor wrapper that intercepts network calls from any application and routes them via Tor.
techniques.notes: More reliable than proxychains for some tools. Blocks UDP (use TCP-mode scanning).

techniques.name: VPN over Tor
techniques.description: Connect VPN through Tor for double anonymization. Hides Tor usage from ISP and hides VPN usage from exit node.
techniques.notes: Significant latency impact. Ensure VPN provider does not log.

techniques.name: Request Timing Jitter
techniques.description: Add randomized delays between requests to mimic human browsing patterns and evade rate-based detection.
techniques.notes: IDS/IPS typically alert on uniform timing. Jitter breaks statistical signatures.

techniques.name: Nmap Slow Timing Templates
techniques.description: Use nmap timing templates T0 (paranoid) to T2 (polite) to minimize detection by IDS/IPS.
techniques.notes: T0 sends one packet every 5 minutes. T1 is 15 seconds between packets. Very slow but nearly undetectable.

techniques.name: Distributed Scanning
techniques.description: Distribute scan traffic across multiple source IPs or time windows to avoid triggering thresholds.

techniques.name: Business Hours Blending
techniques.description: Conduct scans during target organization's business hours to blend with legitimate traffic patterns.
techniques.notes: Traffic during off-hours is more likely to be flagged by anomaly-based detection.

techniques.name: DNS-over-HTTPS (DoH)
techniques.description: Encrypt DNS queries inside HTTPS to prevent ISP/network monitoring of hostname lookups.

techniques.name: DNS-over-Tor
techniques.description: Route DNS queries through the Tor network for maximum privacy. Prevents correlation between DNS queries and browsing.
techniques.notes: Requires Tor SOCKS proxy to be active. Higher latency than DoH.

techniques.name: DNS Leak Prevention
techniques.description: Ensure all DNS queries are routed through the anonymization layer, preventing system resolver from revealing true DNS servers.
techniques.notes: Test with check_anonymity() to detect leaks. Common issue with split-tunnel VPN configs.

techniques.name: Browser UA Rotation
techniques.description: Rotate User-Agent strings across requests to impersonate different browsers and avoid fingerprinting.
techniques.notes: Combine with matching Accept, Accept-Language headers for better realism.

techniques.name: TLS Fingerprint Randomization
techniques.description: Vary TLS ClientHello parameters to evade JA3/JA3S fingerprinting used by advanced WAFs and IDS.
techniques.notes: JA3 fingerprints are tied to TLS library, not just UA string.

techniques.name: Nmap Decoy Scanning
techniques.description: Generate spoofed scan packets from multiple decoy IPs to obscure the true scanner source.
techniques.notes: Requires raw socket privileges. Does not work with connect scan (-sT).

techniques.name: Nmap Fragmentation
techniques.description: Fragment TCP packets to evade packet inspection by IDS/IPS that do not reassemble fragments.

techniques.name: Nmap Source Port Spoofing
techniques.description: Use a common source port (80, 443, 53) to evade firewall rules that permit outbound traffic on these ports.

techniques.name: Nuclei Passive Templates
techniques.description: Run only passive nuclei templates first to gather info without triggering active probes.

techniques.name: Randomized HTTP Headers
techniques.description: Add realistic Accept, Accept-Language, Accept-Encoding headers to make requests appear as legitimate browser traffic.

practices.name: Compartmentalization
practices.description: Use dedicated VMs or containers for pentesting activity. Never mix personal and professional activity on the same session.

practices.name: Minimal Footprint
practices.description: Only run the tools necessary for the current phase. Avoid running multiple aggressive scans simultaneously.

practices.name: Log Review
practices.description: Review your own tool logs to understand what evidence you leave behind. Know what your tools write to disk.

practices.name: Exit Node Country Selection
practices.description: Prefer Tor exit nodes or VPNs in jurisdictions favorable to the engagement. Avoid exit nodes in target country.

practices.name: Identity Rotation Between Phases
practices.description: Request new Tor circuit and rotate UA between major engagement phases to prevent traffic correlation.

practices.name: Pre-Engagement Anonymity Verification
practices.description: Always verify anonymity level before starting active scanning. Use check_anonymity() and tor_check().

practices.name: Audit Log Hygiene
practices.description: Ensure audit logs are stored securely and contain only engagement-relevant data. Review log path permissions.

practices.name: Written Authorization
practices.description: Always obtain written authorization before any active scanning. Keep authorization documents accessible during the engagement.

techniques.name: Payload Encoding
techniques.description: Encode injection payloads (URL, HTML, Unicode, Base64) to bypass signature-based WAF/IDS detection.

techniques.name: Case Variation
techniques.description: Vary case in keywords (SELECT vs SeLeCt) to bypass case-sensitive signature detection in WAFs.

techniques.name: Comment Insertion
techniques.description: Insert SQL/HTML comments mid-keyword (SEL/**/ECT) to break signature patterns.

techniques.name: Connection Throttling
techniques.description: Limit concurrent connections and request rate to stay below IDS thresholds.

techniques.name: Randomized Parameter Ordering
techniques.description: Vary the order of HTTP parameters and headers across requests to avoid statistical detection.

anonymity_levels.none: Direct connection — real IP fully exposed
anonymity_levels.low: Basic proxy — some obfuscation but detectable
anonymity_levels.medium: VPN or single proxy — IP hidden but metadata may leak
anonymity_levels.high: Tor + DoH — strong anonymity, minimal metadata leakage

tengu_stealth_commands: rotate_identity() — new Tor circuit + new UA
